Assignment For Session 3:
The Dream Story


1. Create your own Dream Story using the formula below:

The Dream Story Formula
Follow The Leader
The Specific 'You' and The General 'They'
The Transition Phrase
The Present Tense Promise


Part 1: The Follow The Leader Phrase
This is a simple transition phase to intro into your Dream Story to disengage the viewer's right brain and engage the left. This momentarily places the user into the desire mode.
(Examples: "Imagine this…", "Picture this:", "Can you just see it now?", "Walk with me for a moment as we imagine…")

Your Follow The Leader Phrase:

WRITE IT HERE:



Part 2: The Specific 'You' and 
The General 'They'
This begins by you asking yourself how your product's Primary Benefit plays out in your own life. It's a working example of what your life is like after applying this benefit, which turns into a general example of what the viewer's life will be like after applying the same benefit.

Answer The Following:

WHAT IS YOUR PRIMARY BENEFIT (FROM YOUR USP):


WHEN YOU APPLY THIS BENEFIT YOURSELF, WHAT'S ONE SPECIFIC EXAMPLE OF HOW YOUR DAY OR LIFE LOOKS AFTERWARD?


NOW, TAKE THAT EXAMPLE AND MAKE IT GENERAL SO THAT IT MIGHT APPLY TO ANYONE WATCHING (NOTE: YOU DO NOT USE THE SPECIFIC YOU IN YOUR VSL. THIS IS JUST AN EXERCISE TO GET TO THE GENERAL THEY):



Example (Specific You):
"Waking up on a Sunday morning, excited for the fact I know I get to enjoy slice after slice of real, non-"healthy" Peperoni Pizza-without guilt, while curled up with my girlfriend and watching football all day-and knowing that this is actually helping me get thin and trim!"

Example (General They):
"Waking up and feeling super-excited that you get the joy of eating your favorite foods-whatever they may be-without guilt, and knowing that you will actually get thin and trim because of it! Maybe you want this time to yourself…or maybe you'll share it with a friend, or even a group of friends, to make it that much more fun!"





Part 3: The Transition Phrase
You begin to draw your reader into the logical and applicable by making this more of a left-brain practical experience.
(Examples:  "You know what the BEST part of this experience will be for you?"… or "One of the most awesome things about this is the fact that you…")

Your Transition Phrase:

WRITE IT HERE:


Part 4: The Present Tense Promise
Here, you give your viewer a promise in the present tense of what his/her life will be like after applying this prime benefit.

Examples: 
"You will come home tonight and…"
"Tomorrow night you will be able to walk into any bar in town and pick up the woman you desire." (For a dating product)
"Within a month from now, you will be able to walk into any _______ and (do/feel what?)"

My personal example: 
"You'll know that you are only 24-48 hours away from your favorite foods at all times! That means tomorrow night you can go out and enjoy a treat, knowing that it's empowering you to shed those unwanted pounds! Isn't that a fantastic feeling!"

Your Present Tense Promise:

WRITE IT HERE:



Example: A Completed Dream Story
Just imagine this: Waking up and feeling super-excited that you get the joy of eating your favorite foods-whatever they may be-without guilt, and knowing that you will actually get thin and trim because of it!... 

Maybe you want this time to yourself…or maybe you'll share it with a friend, or even a group of friends, to make it that much more fun!...

And…you know what the BEST part of this experience will be for you?...

You'll know that you are only 24-48 hours away from your favorite foods at all times!... 

That means tomorrow night you can go out and enjoy a treat, knowing that it's empowering you to shed those unwanted pounds!

Isn't that a fantastic feeling! …

Write Your Completed Dream Story Here:
